name: The Clarity Protocol
author: How Good It Can Get
description: A 4-step neuro-pattern interruption methodology for real-time anxiety deconstruction.

steps.name: The Ping
steps.description: Identify the amygdala hardware signal — the physiological sensation before the anxiety story forms. Lasts 5 seconds.

steps.name: The Gap
steps.description: Create a deliberate pause in the reflex loop. The gap interrupts the automatic narrative before it becomes emotion.

steps.name: The Root
steps.description: Collapse the cognitive simulation. Identify the predictive model generating the anxiety and name it as hardware noise, not fact.

steps.name: The Shift
steps.description: Choose a new response. Redirect neural energy toward a deliberate action or thought, completing the interruption cycle.

delivery: Micro-Shift Delivery Method — minutes per day, lasting neural change.
citation: Rice, A. & Maragh, C. (2026). The Clarity Protocol. How Good It Can Get.
